bug_report.summary: Unexpected thin vertical UI artifact appears in right editor pane (Split View area)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application.
Dismiss the update notification dialog (if shown).
Observe the right-side editor pane (Split View) around the mid-upper area while the main window is idle (no Assets window opened).
expected_behaviors: No stray vertical bar/line should appear within the Split View pane content area; the pane should render consistently without artifacts.
actual_behaviors: A thin vertical bar/line appears in the Split View pane area (approx. bbox x=895–905, y=536–582) that was not present before the change.

bug_report.summary: Defaults pane: “Rename file” button is clipped/partially hidden after layout refactor
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application.
Open the Assets/Assets Manager window.
Navigate to the Defaults view (the pane showing a defaults file like HTML.yaml).
Observe the top area of the editor pane where the file name and related controls are shown.
expected_behaviors: The “Rename file” button should be fully visible and not overlapped or clipped by adjacent controls/borders.
actual_behaviors: The “Rename file” button appears pushed to the far right and is partially cut off/overlapped (text and/or button bounds clipped) near the top-right of the Defaults pane.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Defaults pane: “Rename file” control is constrained/partially clipped at top-right after layout refactor (worse in resized/cramped layouts)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application.
Open Assets → Assets Manager.
Go to Defaults (Exporting) so a defaults file (e.g., HTML.yaml) is shown in the right pane.
Resize the Assets Manager window height smaller (cramped height).
Observe the top-right area of the Defaults editor header (where the rename control is shown after the refactor).
expected_behaviors: The “Rename file” button should have adequate padding and be fully visible (no clipping/overlap) across window sizes.
actual_behaviors: The “Rename file” button is pushed into the top-right header area and appears visually cramped/overlapped against adjacent controls/borders, indicating clipping/overflow with the updated layout.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Defaults: Triple-clicking inside the filename field unexpectedly switches the selected defaults entry
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets → Assets Manager.
Go to Defaults (Exporting).
Select an entry (e.g., “Overflow Default 01”) so its name appears in the filename field.
Triple-click inside the filename text field to select the text.
expected_behaviors: Triple-click should only select the filename text within the field; the currently selected defaults entry should remain the same.
actual_behaviors: After triple-clicking the filename field, the selected/defaults entry changes (UI switches to showing a different entry name, e.g., “New Profile 2026-01-05 08:38:41.007.yaml”).

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Defaults: Triple-clicking the filename field changes the selected defaults entry (selection jumps)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets → Assets Manager.
Go to Defaults (Exporting).
Select a defaults item (e.g., “Overflow Default 02”) so its name is shown in the filename field.
Triple-click inside the filename field to select the text.
expected_behaviors: Triple-click selects the text in the filename field for the currently selected item and does not switch which defaults entry is selected/displayed.
actual_behaviors: After triple-clicking, the UI switches to another defaults entry (e.g., “New Profile <timestamp>.yaml”), effectively changing the selection/displayed file unexpectedly.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Defaults editor: “Rename file” control moved into top-right header (next to close X), creating crowded/clipping-prone header and not covered by change intent
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets → Assets Manager.
Go to Defaults (Exporting).
Select any defaults entry so the right editor pane is shown.
Observe where the “Rename file” control appears in the right-pane header area.
expected_behaviors: Unless explicitly intended, “Rename file” should remain in its prior stable position (under/near the filename input) and not be moved into the top-right header control cluster.
Header controls (filename field, close X, Rename) should have sufficient spacing and should not risk overlap/clipping in cramped window sizes or with longer filenames.
actual_behaviors: “Rename file” appears in the top-right header area next to the close (X) control (instead of under/near the filename field as before).
This compresses the header control cluster and increases the likelihood of overlap/clipping with long filenames or constrained window sizes.

false_positive_reasons: Expected GUI difference due to intended code change

bug_report.summary: Defaults: Triple-clicking inside the filename field unexpectedly changes the selected defaults entry (selection jumps to another file)
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets → Assets Manager.
Go to Defaults (Exporting).
Select an entry such as “Overflow Default 03” so its name is shown in the filename field in the right pane.
Triple-click inside the filename field to select the text.
expected_behaviors: Triple-click should select the filename text in the field for the currently selected entry.
The selected defaults entry should not change just from selecting text in the filename field.
actual_behaviors: After triple-clicking, the displayed filename/selected entry changes to another defaults file (e.g., “New Profile <timestamp>.yaml”), indicating an unexpected selection change.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Defaults: Typing into the filename field unexpectedly switches the loaded defaults file/content in the editor
steps_to_reproduce: Open Assets → Assets Manager.
Go to Defaults (Exporting).
Select a defaults entry so its content is shown in the right editor (e.g., a YAML defaults file).
Click into the filename field at the top of the right pane and type a new name (e.g., “Overflow Default 04”).
expected_behaviors: Typing in the filename field should only edit the filename text and keep the currently loaded defaults file/content displayed in the editor until an explicit rename/confirm action occurs.
actual_behaviors: After typing in the filename field, the right editor content changes to a different defaults file/template view (e.g., showing the “This is a new defaults file…” template), implying an unintended selection/content switch triggered by typing.

bug_report.summary: Closing an open menu with Escape unexpectedly changes editor viewport/scroll position and word count (focus jumps)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application.
Open any top menu (e.g., File/Edit/View/Window/Help).
Ensure an editor pane is scrolled to a non-top position (e.g., mid-document).
Press Escape to close the menu.
expected_behaviors: Only the menu closes.
The active editor pane/document remains the same.
Editor scroll position/viewport does not change.
Word-count indicator remains consistent with the active document/pane before closing the menu.
actual_behaviors: After closing the menu, the lower-right editor pane content jumps to a different scroll position (appears to reset toward the top of the document, showing the “Helpful Links” heading instead of the previously visible mid-document section).
The word-count indicator changes (e.g., from 61 words to 1,596 words), suggesting the active pane/document focus changed unexpectedly.

bug_report.summary: Interacting with Assets Manager (switching to Snippets tab) changes main window word count/active document unexpectedly
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Assets Manager window.
Observe the main window word count indicator.
Click the “Snippets” tab within Assets Manager.
Observe the main window word count indicator again.
expected_behaviors: Interacting with the Assets Manager (e.g., switching tabs) should not change the active document/pane in the main window, and the word-count indicator should remain consistent.
actual_behaviors: While switching to the Snippets tab, the main window word-count indicator changes (e.g., from “61 words” to “1,596 words”), suggesting background focus/active document changes unexpectedly.

bug_report.summary: Assets Manager (Snippets): snippet list column is too narrow, causing excessive name truncation despite available space
steps_to_reproduce: Open File → Preferences… → Assets Manager.
Go to the “Snippets” tab.
Create a new snippet and rename it to a longer name (e.g., “Save Timing Test 01”).
Observe the snippet entry in the left snippet list.
expected_behaviors: The snippet list (SelectableList) should use available width appropriately (or be resizable) so snippet names are not excessively truncated when there is ample dialog space.
actual_behaviors: The snippet list column remains very narrow and the snippet name is truncated with an ellipsis (e.g., “Save-Timing-Test...”), even though there is significant unused horizontal space in the window.

bug_report.summary: Assets Manager: save-status indicator (“Unsaved changes”) is rendered as a prominent boxed label and positioned inconsistently (more flashy)
steps_to_reproduce: Open File → Preferences… → Assets Manager.
Go to the Snippets tab.
Make an edit in the snippet editor so the state becomes unsaved (e.g., press Enter to add a new line).
Observe the save-status indicator near the Save button.
expected_behaviors: Save-status text should be subtle/consistent with the intent to make status feedback less flashy.
Save-status indicator placement should be consistent across Assets Manager tabs (e.g., Snippets vs Custom CSS).
actual_behaviors: The “Unsaved changes” indicator is shown in a prominent boxed/bordered label next to the Save button, making it visually louder than a simple text label.
The indicator’s placement/styling differs from other tabs (e.g., appears as plain text under Save in one tab vs boxed inline in another).

bug_report.summary: Unintended visual change to editor pane vertical scrollbar (thumb/track styling or visibility) after CSS Assets window refactor
steps_to_reproduce: Open the application.
Open the View menu (as in the screenshot).
Look at the vertical scrollbar on the right side of the upper-right editor pane (Split View).
expected_behaviors: Editor pane scrollbar appearance/visibility should remain unchanged by Assets window CSS/layout changes.
Scrollbar thumb/track should render consistently and clearly indicate scroll position.
actual_behaviors: The vertical scrollbar area in the upper-right editor pane renders differently (thumb/track appearance/position changes) in the after-change build (difference highlighted around x=895–905, y=536–582).
